question: Structural spec calls for 5,000 psi concrete but drawing notes show 4,000 psi. Please confirm.
draft:
**TO:** Design Team / Architect of Record  
**FROM:** VDC Coordination Team  
**DATE:** 2026-04-25  
**RE:** Response to RFI-TEST-001  

---

**QUESTION:**  
Structural spec calls for 5,000 psi concrete but drawing notes show 4,000 psi. Please confirm.

**RESPONSE:**  
We have reviewed the structural specifications and note that concrete strength requirements vary by element. Per SECTION 03 30 00 - CAST-IN-PLACE CONCRETE, columns and post-tensioned slabs require 5,000 psi minimum at 28 days, while floor slabs require 4,000 psi minimum at 28 days. Please confirm which specific element or location the drawing note refers to, so we can address the discrepancy accordingly.

**REFERENCES:**  
- PROJECT: DOWNTOWN OFFICE TOWER - STRUCTURAL SPECIFICATIONS, SECTION 03 30 00 - CAST-IN-PLACE CONCRETE

---

**PREPARED BY:** VDC Document Intelligence AI  
**REVIEW STATUS:** Draft - Subject to VDC Manager Review

sources.text:
PROJECT: DOWNTOWN OFFICE TOWER - STRUCTURAL SPECIFICATIONS
SECTION 03 30 00 - CAST-IN-PLACE CONCRETE

1.1 GENERAL
All concrete work shall comply with ACI 318-19 and local building codes. 1.2 CONCRETE MIX DESIGNS
- Footings: 3,000 psi minimum at 28 days
- Columns: 5,000 psi minimum at 28 days  
- Floor slabs: 4,000 psi minimum at 28 days
- Post-tensioned slabs: 5,000 psi minimum at 28 days, high ea

PROJECT: DOWNTOWN OFFICE TOWER - STRUCTURAL SPECIFICATIONS
SECTION 03 30 00 - CAST-IN-PLACE CONCRETE

1.1 GENERAL
All concrete work shall comply with ACI 318-19 and local building codes. 1.2 CONCRETE MIX DESIGNS
- Footings: 3,000 psi minimum at 28 days
- Columns: 5,000 psi minimum at 28 days  
- Floor slabs: 4,000 psi minimum at 28 days
- Post-tensioned slabs: 5,000 psi minimum at 28 days, high ea

1.4 FIREPROOFING
Structural steel columns and beams shall receive 2-hour fireproofing per IBC Table 601. Fireproofing material: Spray-applied cementitious, minimum 1.5 inches thick

1.5 STRUCTURAL STEEL
Wide flange beams: ASTM A992, Grade 50
Connection design per AISC 360-16

1.6 DEAD LOADS
Floor finish: 2 psf
Partitions: 15 psf (movable)
Mechanical/electrical: 10 psf
Ceiling: 3 psf

1.7 LIVE LOAD

1.4 FIREPROOFING
Structural steel columns and beams shall receive 2-hour fireproofing per IBC Table 601. Fireproofing material: Spray-applied cementitious, minimum 1.5 inches thick

1.5 STRUCTURAL STEEL
Wide flange beams: ASTM A992, Grade 50
Connection design per AISC 360-16

1.6 DEAD LOADS
Floor finish: 2 psf
Partitions: 15 psf (movable)
Mechanical/electrical: 10 psf
Ceiling: 3 psf

1.7 LIVE LOAD

Please advise on structural accommodation or equipment relocation. RFI-003: Window U-factor in architectural notes shows 0.30, but energy model requires 0.28 for code compliance. Please confirm if window specification will be updated. RFI-004: Fire damper locations not shown on mechanical drawings for shaft penetrations. Please provide shop drawings with damper schedule. RFI-005: Lobby floor finis
